name: Slider
description: Controlled numeric slider for selecting one value or a two-thumb range by pointer drag or keyboard.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class for the root element

states.rationale: Slider track uses semantic/intent token swaps for disabled state and no overlay or OKLCH interactive state system. Thumb hover, focus, active, and disabled states are owned by SliderItem; disabled Slider passes disabled to SliderItem, matching Figma SliderItem state=disabled.

keyboard.ArrowLeft: Decrements the focused thumb by step
keyboard.ArrowDown: Decrements the focused thumb by step
keyboard.ArrowRight: Increments the focused thumb by step
keyboard.ArrowUp: Increments the focused thumb by step
keyboard.PageDown: Decrements the focused thumb by step * 10
keyboard.PageUp: Increments the focused thumb by step * 10
keyboard.Home: Moves the focused thumb to the lower bound
keyboard.End: Moves the focused thumb to the upper bound

accessibility.focusManagement: Natural tab order on enabled SliderItem thumbs; disabled thumbs use tabIndex=-1

propertyMapping.note: Figma boolean controls visual mode. Code uses the presence of the numeric range tuple for range mode.
Code-only numeric value for single mode.
Code-only on the Slider Figma set. Disabled thumb visuals are covered by related SliderItem state=disabled (6618:12103).

useWhen: Adjusting settings like volume, brightness, price, or thresholds
Users benefit from quick visual fine-tuning
doNotUseFor: Precise typed numeric entry - use InputField
Unbounded values or values without clear min/max

knownDeviations.description: No L4 Slider color tokens in tokens.json. Track uses L2 semantic and intent tokens directly; radius uses the shared SliderItem radius token.
Figma range? is a boolean visual variant, while React range is a numeric tuple that controls both mode and values.
Slider Figma set has no whole-component disabled variant. Disabled thumb visuals are represented by SliderItem state=disabled; disabled track styling remains Slider code/documentation.
Figma examples are fixed at 400px. Code is fluid width and relies on the parent container for sizing.
